LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS' KENNETH SIMONCINI, SIMONCINI & ASSOCIATES, KERRI JOHNSON AND JOYCE CLIFFORD'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SAC) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. LIKE AN ACTION FOR FRAUD, CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD MUST BE PLED WITH SPECIFICITY. (TINDELL V. MURPHY (2018) 22 CAL.APP.5TH 1239.) THIS THE SAC DOES NOT DO. AS TO DECLARATORY RELIEF, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CLAIM A CONTINUING RELATIONSHIP JUSTIFYING A DECLARATION OF FUTURE RIGHTS. (SEE OPP. 13:15-19; ROBERTS V. LOS ANGELES COUNTY BAR ASSN. (2003) 105 CAL.APP.4TH 604, 618.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendants Kenneth Simoncini, Simoncini & Associates, Kerri Johnson and Joyce Clifford's motion to seal.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Kenneth Simoncini, Simoncini & Associates, Kerri Johnson and Joyce Clifford's motion to seal, which is unopposed is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The court denies the request to seal the FAC and SAC in their entirety. The court grants the request to seal the portions of the FAC and SAC identified in the moving papers. Defendants are to work with the clerk to ensure the proposed order is appropriate and accomplishes the ends of sealing the requested portions of the FAC and SAC.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Adam Gruen, Esq. 510-685-6863 for Plaintiff.
Kristin L. Iversen, Esq. (415) 788-1900, MURPHY, PEARSON, BRADLEY & FEENEY for DEFENDANTS KENNETH SIMONCINI, SIMONCINI & ASSOCIATES, KERRI JOHNSON, JOYCE CLIFFORD.

RULING - A R G U E D. Parties inform the Court the Demurrer and Motion to strike are not contested. Ms. Iverson only seeks clarification on the motion to strike. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS' KENNETH SIMONCINI, SIMONCINI & ASSOCIATES, KERRI JOHNSON and JOYCE CLIFFORD'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is sustained with leave to amend. Like an action for fraud, constructive fraud must be pled with specificity. (Tindell v. Murphy (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1239.) This the SAC does not do. As to declaratory relief, plaintiff does not claim a continuing relationship justifying a declaration of future rights. (See Opp. 13:15-19; Roberts v. Los Angeles County Bar Assn. (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 604, 618.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Adam Gruen, Esq. 510-685-6863 for Plaintiff.
Kristin L. Iversen, Esq. (415) 788-1900, MURPHY, PEARSON, BRADLEY & FEENEY for DEFENDANTS KENNETH SIMONCINI, SIMONCINI & ASSOCIATES, KERRI JOHNSON, JOYCE CLIFFORD.

RULING - A R G U E D. Parties inform the Court the Demurrer and Motion to strike are not contested. Ms. Iverson only seeks clarification on the motion to strike. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS KENNETH SIMONCINI, SIMONCINI & ASSOCIATES, KERRI JOHNSON and JOYCE CLIFFORD Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint.

Given sustaining of defendants' demurrer, the SAC's constructive trust allegations are stricken with leave to amend. At the pleading stage, the SAC's allegations support a prayer for punitive damages (see, e.g., id. 5:13-12:26) and that part of the motion is thus denied.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS KENNETH SIMONCINI, SIMONCINI & ASSOCIATES, KERRI JOHNSON, AND JOYCE CLIFFORD'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Kenneth Simoncini et al's motion to strike is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by March 02, 2023.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Its [Proposed] First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF METRO SERVICES GROUP's Motion for Leave to File Its [Proposed] First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) Though cases go both ways, the better view is that a proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) Defendants may file pleadings motions if they wish. Prejudice to defendants is slight; trial is more than a year off and plaintiff reports discovery is in its infancy.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
